name: client
context: Generate and use the Prisma Client to perform type-safe database queries, mutations, and transactions
info: Prisma Client is a type-safe database client auto-generated from your schema. After running `npx prisma generate`, you get a `PrismaClient` class with methods for every model: `findUnique`, `findFirst`, `findMany`, `create`, `createMany`, `update`, `updateMany`, `upsert`, `delete`, `deleteMany`, `count`, `aggregate`, and `groupBy`. The client supports nested writes, relation loading via `include` and `select`, raw SQL via `$queryRaw` and `$executeRaw`, and transactions via `$transaction`. In production, use a singleton pattern to avoid exhausting database connections. See [[prisma/client/queries]] for CRUD operations, [[prisma/client/relations]] for relation queries, and [[prisma/troubleshooting]] for error handling.

sections.title: Setup and generation
sections.body: Install `@prisma/client` (runtime) and `prisma` (dev). Run `npx prisma generate` after every schema change to regenerate the client. The client is generated into `node_modules/.prisma/client` by default. Import from `@prisma/client`. The `migrate dev` command auto-runs `generate`.

sections.title: Singleton pattern
sections.body: Create a single `PrismaClient` instance and reuse it across your application. In Next.js and other hot-reloading frameworks, store the instance on `globalThis` to prevent connection pool exhaustion during development. Each `PrismaClient` instance opens its own connection pool.

sections.title: Transactions
sections.body: Use `prisma.$transaction([...])` for sequential batched operations or `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { ... })` for interactive transactions with custom logic and rollback. Transaction options: `maxWait` (ms to wait for slot), `timeout` (ms max execution), `isolationLevel` (ReadUncommitted, ReadCommitted, RepeatableRead, Serializable). Throwing inside an interactive transaction triggers automatic rollback.

sections.title: Raw SQL queries
sections.body: Use `prisma.$queryRaw` with tagged template literals for parameterized SELECT queries. Use `prisma.$executeRaw` for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE. Both use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Avoid `$queryRawUnsafe` and `$executeRawUnsafe` with user input.

sections.title: Error handling
sections.body: Prisma throws typed errors: `PrismaClientKnownRequestError` (has `.code` like P2002 for unique violation), `PrismaClientValidationError` (invalid query), `PrismaClientInitializationError` (connection failure). Catch specific error codes for business logic (e.g., P2002 = duplicate, P2025 = not found). See [[prisma/troubleshooting]] for the full error code reference.
